title: Hieracium lividorubens
taxonomy: Hieracium lividorubens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion-like hawkweed group. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises over 1000 species of plants. The genus Hieracium is part of the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This classification places Hieracium lividorubens within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Its classification is also consistent with the broader taxonomic classification of plants that includes kingdom Plantae.
uses: Hieracium lividorubens is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, information on its specific medicinal uses is limited and more research is needed to confirm its benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have been used in traditional folk medicine, but the exact uses are not well-documented. Some sources suggest that it may have anti-inflammatory properties, although this has not been extensively studied. Further research is required to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Hieracium lividorubens.
properties:
Hieracium lividorubens, a plant species also known as the livid red hawkweed, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, limited information is available regarding its specific therapeutic properties. 

More research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and applications of Hieracium lividorubens in traditional medicine. Some studies suggest that plants in the Hieracium genus may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. But, more in-depth research is required to confirm the specific therapeutic properties of this particular species.
constituents: Hieracium lividorubens contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones as its active constituents, contributing to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in the plant may also possess antiviral and anti-cancer properties. Sesquiterpene lactones are known for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and have been studied for their potential therapeutic applications. Further research is necessary to fully understand the range of bioactive compounds present in Hieracium lividorubens.
parts: I couldn't find information about the medicinal parts of Hieracium lividorubens.
side_effects: I couldn't find any information on Hieracium lividorubens side effects, but Hieracium species are generally not recommended for internal consumption due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which can cause gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. Ingesting large quantities of Hieracium species may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The exact side effects of Hieracium lividorubens are not well-documented, but caution is advised when handling or consuming any part of the plant. As with any new substance, consult a medical professional before using Hieracium lividorubens for medicinal purposes. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Hieracium lividorubens.
intro: Hieracium lividorubens, commonly known as yellow hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium lividorubens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium lividorubens is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
